module.title: SAP TM Implementation, Methodology & Best Practices

learningObjectives.title: Understand deployment transition
learningObjectives.description: Explain cutover preparation, go-live execution, and production stabilization principles.

learningObjectives.title: Understand readiness and control
learningObjectives.description: Identify checklist controls for migration, validation, security, and business readiness.

learningObjectives.title: Understand hypercare importance
learningObjectives.description: Describe post-go-live intensive support and successful support-team handover.

sections.content: The Cutover, Go-Live and Hypercare phases represent the final transition from project implementation to production operations. A well-planned cutover ensures business continuity, while Hypercare provides intensive support immediately after Go-Live to quickly resolve production issues and stabilize the SAP TM environment.

sections.title: Key Concept
sections.content: Go-Live is not the end of an SAP TM project. Hypercare is essential to stabilize the production system before transferring ownership to the support organization.

sections.title: Deployment Activities
rows: Load production master and transactional data
Switch business operations to SAP TM

sections.content: Before Go-Live, the implementation team completes master data migration, validates interfaces with SAP S/4HANA and SAP EWM, and obtains business approval. After production deployment, consultants remain available during a three-week Hypercare period to resolve transportation planning, integration and settlement issues before handing the solution to the support team.

sections.title: Best Practice
sections.content: Create a detailed cutover plan with task owners, dependencies, timelines and rollback procedures. Conduct at least one mock cutover before the actual Go-Live.

sections.title: Real Project Example
sections.content: A multinational manufacturer performed two mock cutovers before production deployment. During Hypercare, consultants monitored transportation planning and integration around the clock, resolving critical issues within hours and ensuring uninterrupted logistics operations.

sections.title: Interview Tip
sections.content: Know the sequence: Cutover Planning → Data Migration → Go-Live → Hypercare → Production Support. Interviewers frequently ask about the purpose of Hypercare.

sections.title: Common Mistake
sections.content: Ending consultant involvement immediately after Go-Live is risky. Hypercare is necessary to identify and resolve production issues before handing the system to the long-term support team.

items: Cutover prepares the organization for production deployment.
Go-Live transitions business operations to SAP TM.
A detailed cutover checklist reduces deployment risks.
Successful Hypercare leads to stable production operations and a smooth support handover.
